summary: vocab-disjoint claim survives (0 unresolved) but framing overreaches; realistic worst case is cross-surface

details.target: methodological claim: vocab-disjoint is the worst-case ISA-HiddenState evaluation for the realistic attacker on AloePri

decomposed_points.label: residual blind ~unchanged across splits
decomposed_points.attack_claim: vocab-disjoint is not a stronger worst case; blind multi-key recovery unchanged across splits
decomposed_points.evidence: aloepri_multikey_blind.json: keymat L0 blind 0.064 disjoint vs 0.060 rowsplit
decomposed_points.recommended_fix: state the residual blind result is split-insensitive (~0.06 both ways)

decomposed_points.label: matched ceiling high => info preserved
decomposed_points.attack_claim: AloePri has not destroyed residual token info; matched recovery stays high
decomposed_points.evidence: matched L0 0.995 rowsplit / 0.914 disjoint; matched is out-of-model ceiling, blind is in-model
decomposed_points.recommended_fix: scope 'defends' to the in-model blind attacker; call it access control not information destruction

decomposed_points.label: vocab-disjoint wrong realistic residual headline
decomposed_points.attack_claim: vocab-disjoint deletes the frequent tokens a realistic attacker learns first; wrong headline split
decomposed_points.evidence: private-rag aloepri-attacks.md: row-split is the realistic null; BUT pythia residual blind is ~0.06 under row-split too, so the low number is not caused by disjoint subtraction
decomposed_points.recommended_fix: report both splits; row-split as the realistic shared-vocab headline, vocab-disjoint as the generalization stress test

decomposed_points.label: tau-leak is real but wire-surface
decomposed_points.attack_claim: attacker is not fully blind over tau; TFMA/SDA + specials leak high-frequency tokens that dominate prompt recovery
decomposed_points.evidence: aloepri_tokenid_sweep.json surface=token_id_api, TFMA 0.52 / SDA 0.75 at eps1=inf; Pi is activation-inert (static-obf.html) so it does not bear on the RESIDUAL split
decomposed_points.recommended_fix: compose surfaces: realistic worst case = tau-leak-weighted per-token recovery across wire + residual

decomposed_points.label: partial-tau does not break residual blind
decomposed_points.attack_claim: a partial-tau memorizing attacker invalidates the residual result and recovers unharvested tokens
decomposed_points.evidence: aloepri-attacks.md: ISA observes State_d, never sees tau; <=20-313 pairs far below ridge bootstrap and do not span natural-language tokens

details.net_assessment: Claim not killed (0 unresolved): residual conclusion sound and split-insensitive (~0.06 both splits). But the 'worst case for the realistic attacker' framing overreaches; realistic leverage is a partial-tau WIRE channel (TFMA/SDA) for a <=20-293 token subset, not a residual memorization channel. Neither split is universally worst case; correct measure is a cross-surface tau-leak-weighted composite.
top_action_items: Report residual ISA-HiddenState as BOTH row-split and vocab-disjoint (row-split realistic headline, disjoint stress test); note they are ~equal for the blind attack.
Add a composite realistic-attacker metric: per-token recovery weighted by a tau-leak prior across wire (TFMA/SDA) + residual blind.
Add a partial-tau curve (0,20,293,313,1024 known pairs) measuring harvested-token recovery and generalization to unharvested tokens.
